channel.title: MBallSectionsDR
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from MBallSectionsDR, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Gallup’s Presidential Approval Misstep
item.description: The polling industry seems increasingly uninterested in its own history.

item.title: In battleground Michigan, 3 Democrats test vision of affordability in the Senate primary
item.description: When Donald Trump fought his way back to the White House in 2024, he capitalized on simmering economic discontent in political battlegrounds such as M...

item.title: How Jeffrey Epstein used the glamour of the Nobel Peace Prize to entice his global network of elites
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein repeatedly played up his ties to the former head of the Nobel Peace Prize committee in invitations to and chats with elites like Richa...

item.title: Minnesota shooting of Venezuelan man is the latest where video evidence contradicts ICE accounts
item.description: Federal authorities announced an investigation Friday into two immigration officers who appeared to have made untruthful statements under oath about a...

item.title: Feds open a perjury probe into ICE officers' testimony about the shooting of a Venezuelan man
item.description: Federal authorities have opened a criminal probe into whether two immigration officers lied under oath about a shooting in Minneapolis last month, as ...

item.title: The Ugly Deportation Case of Rumeysa Öztürk
item.description: The administration may appeal the latest decision and might well win. But its conduct has conveyed weakness in several respects.

item.title: New York’s Museum of Sex: Serious, Sly, Fun, and Funky
item.description: A collection on sex through the ages explores its power, delights — oh, and there’s plenty of paraphernalia.

item.title: Trump Should End the FDA’s 25-Year Cover-Up on the Abortion Pill
item.description: Mifepristone has not gotten safer; the FDA has just gotten more political.

item.title: From Chuck To AOC, Here Are The Worst People Of The Week
item.description: From Chuck Schumer’s race-baiting false equivalencies to Lisa Murkowski being, well, Lisa Murkowski, it was a great week for bad people.

item.title: The Nixon Library Is Wrong About Nixon And The Deep State
item.description: The new revelation about Nixon 'bears directly on allegations by President Trump and his supporters about the existence of what was once called the pe...

item.title: AI and Our Collapsing Creative Horizons
item.description: The real danger AI poses is to original thought itself.

item.title: Here Are 10 Great Justice Scalia Quotes To Mark A Decade Since His Passing
item.description: Here are some of Justice Scalia's best moments in remembrance of the 10-year anniversary of his passing.

item.title: Virginia High Court Greenlights Democrat Effort To Give Millions Of Republicans A Single Seat In Congress
item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court handed Democrats a massive win Friday, allowing them to move forward with a redistricting referendum that would gerrymander...

item.title: Judge rules ICE can't make warrantless arrests at some churches
item.description: A federal judge said Friday that ICE's policy of allowing arrests at churches tramples on religious rights, and he issued an order blocking arrests un...

item.title: Turns Out The Irishman Detained By ICE Was Facing Drug Charges Back Home
item.description: The arrest of Irishman Seamus Culleton fueled a false media narrative that ICE is grabbing law-abiding people who have done nothing wrong.
